system_description.overview: EverCurrent is an AI-native platform of record that aligns tools, processes, and knowledge for hardware teams. It provides automated documentation of changes, a cross-functional knowledgebase, and risk/critical path analysis to accelerate hardware development.

products.Automated Documentation of Changes: AI agents monitor project activities across CAD, PLM, project management, and communication tools. Every change is automatically logged, versioned, and tied to its originating source with full traceability.
products.Cross-Functional Knowledgebase: Unified repository that brings together information from email, Slack, CAD, PLM, and shared drives. AI agents parse unstructured content into structured, searchable records with role-based access.
products.Risk & Critical Path Analysis: AI models continuously scan project data for risks, dependencies, and bottlenecks. Critical paths are visualized through Gantt charts and timeline dashboards with predictive insights.

infrastructure.physical_presence: None - no physical offices or servers; all data processed on cloud services

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with access control lists (ACLs). Public subnet contains Load Balancer; Private subnet contains Application Layer (Frontend + Backend); Data Layer in separate segment within the VPC (VPC: 10.0.0.0/16).
network_architecture.api_gateway: Application gateway with firewalls configured on it
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on the application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access through a bastion host with a virtual private network (VPN)
network_architecture.tls: TLS using certificates from an established third-party certificate authority for all web-based systems
network_architecture.content_filtering: All systems restricted to the internet with content filtering system routed through proxy server
network_architecture.topology: Users -> Internet -> AWS Production VPC (10.0.0.0/16) -> Public Subnet (Load Balancer) -> Private Subnet Application Layer (React Frontend + FastAPI Python Backend) -> Data Layer (PostgreSQL + Weaviate vector database). External services include Auth0, OpenAI, and Google APIs. CI/CD via GitHub Actions for automated deployment.

application_architecture.pattern: Hybrid (Frontend/Backend split with separate data layer)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments (CC8.1.4 confirms changes developed and tested in separate environment)

data_storage.backup_frequency: Not explicitly specified; formal backup policy exists with procedures defined in Backup Policy

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: Auth0 (shown in network diagram as third-party API)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform (CC6.1.1); also required for source code tool access (CC8.1.3); two-factor authentication required for email access
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination of employment
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through Password policy of AWS including periodic forced changes, password expiry, and complexity requirements. Accounts disabled after limited number of unsuccessful logon attempts.

encryption.key_management: Cloud provider key management service (AWS KMS implied)

ci_cd_devops.source_control: GitHub (with source code management tool, MFA access per CC8.1.3)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to move changes to production requires additional approval (CC8.1.2)
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for baseline configurations retained as code, with rollback capability (CC7.1.1)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management with monthly patch management reviews by CTO. Workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned for patch compliance daily (CC7.5.1).

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named; monitoring tools used to continuously monitor system capacity and performance (CC5.2.1)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Not named; logging enabled to monitor administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions at application and infrastructure level (CC7.2.1)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events (CC7.2.1). Monitoring tool generates alerts when predefined thresholds are met (CC5.2.1).

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure and data center services (subservice organization)
AI/ML API services for platform intelligence features
Source code management and CI/CD (GitHub Actions)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software installed on all production servers (continuous auto-scan), Mac workstations and laptops. Virus definitions updated weekly for production servers, daily for endpoints (CC6.8.1).
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications, frequency adjusted as required (CC7.1.2)
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party. Results reviewed by IT management, high/critical findings remediated per incident response plan (CC4.1.1).

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access to facilities: AWS responsible for physical security of data center; not in scope
A1.2 - Environmental protections: EverCurrent operates from cloud/coworking spaces; environmental controls managed by building management/AWS
Processing Integrity - Determined not relevant to the system
Privacy - Determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: CC8.1.2: Branch protection rules require additional approval for every merge to production - no direct developer commits to prod
CC6.1.3: Customer access appropriately segmented from other customer accounts (per-tenant segregation)
CC7.4.1: BCDR plans tested annually per defined schedule for different loss scenarios with results reviewed and plans updated
CC9.2.1-CC9.2.3: Comprehensive vendor management with SOC report reviews, quarterly risk assessments for critical vendors without SOC reports, and formal vendor termination procedures
CC4.1.1: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party with remediation tracking

infrastructure.rationale: Single-region AWS deployment with Multi-AZ. VPC with proper subnet segmentation. No CDN, no multi-region, no serverless mentioned. IaC implied through configuration management tool but not explicitly named (e.g., Terraform). Basic but functional cloud setup.

application_architecture.rationale: Clear frontend/backend separation with React and FastAPI (typed Python framework). Interesting use of Weaviate vector database for AI features. Branch protection and code review enforced. However, no mention of automated testing, container orchestration, or microservices. Relatively straightforward two-tier app architecture.

data_layer.rationale: PostgreSQL as primary database plus Weaviate vector DB shows thoughtful data architecture for an AI product. Multi-AZ deployment. Data classification and retention policies exist. Per-tenant segregation confirmed. However, no caching layer, no data warehouse, backup frequency not specified, and no specific mention of S3 or file storage.

security_posture.rationale: Good fundamentals: MFA for admin and source code access, IDS/IPS via AWS, annual pen testing, monthly vulnerability scans, endpoint protection with anti-malware, server hardening, encryption at rest and in transit. Missing: no WAF mentioned, no named SIEM tool, no named monitoring/APM tools, TLS version not specified, no cybersecurity insurance mentioned.

devops_maturity.rationale: GitHub + GitHub Actions for CI/CD is a solid modern choice. Branch protection with required approvals. Separate dev/test and production environments. Emergency change process documented. Configuration management as code with rollback. However, no container orchestration, IaC tool not named, no evidence of automated test pipelines.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, documented and reviewed annually. Annual DR testing with defined scenarios. Multi-AZ failover. However, no multi-region failover, RTO and RPO not disclosed, backup frequency not specified. The policy exists and is tested but lacks specificity on recovery targets.

vendor_diversity.rationale: 5 identifiable vendors (AWS, Auth0, OpenAI, Google APIs, GitHub). However, extremely heavy AWS dependency for all infrastructure. Auth0 is a single point for authentication. OpenAI dependency for core AI features. Vendor management policy is strong with annual SOC reviews and quarterly assessments, but the actual vendor concentration is a moderate risk.

overall.rationale: EverCurrent demonstrates a solid baseline for an early-stage AI/hardware-dev company achieving its first SOC 2 Type 1. The architecture is modern (FastAPI + React + vector DB) with good security fundamentals (MFA, IDS/IPS, pen testing, vulnerability scanning). The report is clean with no exceptions. Key gaps include lack of specificity on many tools (monitoring, SIEM, IaC), no multi-region capability, undisclosed RTO/RPO, and heavy single-vendor concentration on AWS. The Type 1 status means operating effectiveness has not yet been tested over time.

red_flags: CEO/CTO is the same person (Ye Wang) - single point of failure for both strategic and technical leadership, and also approves IS policy changes and incident response actions, creating potential segregation of duties concern
No WAF (Web Application Firewall) mentioned despite being a web-based SaaS application
RTO and RPO not disclosed - unclear recovery time commitments to customers
No named SIEM or centralized log management solution despite having logging controls
Processing Integrity trust criteria excluded - concerning for a platform that processes engineering/hardware development data where data accuracy is critical
yellow_flags: Type 1 report only - controls design assessed but operating effectiveness not yet tested over a period
Many security tools unnamed (monitoring, SIEM, APM, IaC) - could indicate immature tooling or deliberate vagueness
No multi-region capability - single region failure could impact all customers
No caching layer mentioned - potential performance concerns at scale
No container orchestration - may limit scalability
Backup frequency not explicitly stated despite having a backup policy
Very small engineering team structure (CEO/CTO -> Engineering Lead -> Backend Engineer + Frontend Engineer) - bus factor risk
TLS version not specified - could be running older TLS versions
green_flags: Clean audit opinion with zero exceptions across all tested controls
Modern tech stack: FastAPI (Python) + React + PostgreSQL + Weaviate vector DB + GitHub Actions
Network diagram shows clear segmentation: public subnet (LB) -> private subnet (app layer) -> data layer within VPC
MFA enforced for production admin access AND source code tool access
Branch protection with required code review approvals before production merge (CC8.1.2)
Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party with formal remediation tracking
Monthly automated vulnerability scanning with frequency adjusted as needed
Comprehensive vendor management program with SOC report reviews and quarterly risk assessments for vendors without SOC
BCDR plan tested annually with defined loss scenarios
Formal change management process with emergency change procedures
Access revoked within one business day of termination with employee termination checklist
Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including admin (CC6.2.2)
key_observations: The company describes itself as an 'AI-automated compliance platform' in the system description but the product section focuses on hardware development tooling - the SOC 2 report itself may be partially a demonstration of their own compliance automation capabilities

Weaviate vector database inclusion signals meaningful AI/ML architecture rather than superficial AI branding - the company is genuinely building AI-native infrastructure for their knowledgebase and risk analysis features

The very small team size (4 visible roles in org chart) combined with a successful SOC 2 audit suggests either heavy automation of compliance processes or significant use of external compliance consultants
Auth0 usage for authentication rather than building custom auth is a positive signal for a small team - shows pragmatic build-vs-buy decisions

The carve-out method for AWS means EverCurrent is explicitly relying on AWS's own SOC 2 controls for physical/environmental security - appropriate for a cloud-native company but means the full security picture requires reviewing AWS's SOC report as well

Content filtering through proxy server is an unusual control for a modern cloud-native startup - may indicate use of a compliance template or more mature security practices than typical for the company size
